source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Talos ERT LLC OCS-G02280 SM 130 B Facility Fire

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - South Marsh Island Block 130

event.incident_type: Fire and Explosion from Pipeline Failure
actions_taken: Instructions given to remaining platforms in SM 130 field to shut in
Non-essential personnel mustered across bridge to SM 130 D facility
Fire hose used to soak smoldering boards under wet oil tank
Four 30 lb. dry chemical fire extinguishers used to extinguish lifesaving equipment and other items
event.summary: On December 15, 2023, at approximately 0520 hours, an explosion and fire occurred on the Talos ERT LLC OCS-G02280, South Marsh Island 130 B Facility. A 3-foot section of an 8-inch bi-directional gas pipeline (Segment 5114) containing 1220 psi failed due to severe external corrosion of a flange. The resulting fire reached approximately 80 feet high from below the +10 level to the production deck. The flame lasted 5-7 minutes before gas pressure bled down. There was damage to life saving equipment, wiring, and minor structural damage near the +10 level. No injuries occurred. High winds (28 mph) blew the flame away from the platform, potentially preventing more severe damage. Investigation revealed the flange failed due to severe OD corrosion driven by high chlorine concentrations (12,000 ppm) in an oxygen-containing environment, with inadequate coating maintenance and incomplete mechanical integrity inspections.
key_phrases: wall loss and material property degradation
rope access was needed but not performed
mechanical integrity test required every 5 years

controls.name: Protective coating on pipeline flange
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier against corrosive environment

supporting_text: The flange had been painted for corrosion protection; however, the coating was not maintained overtime to prevent corrosion of the underlying steel when exposed.

controls.name: 5-year mechanical integrity inspection program
controls.barrier_role: Detect and assess pipeline degradation before failure
controls.lod_basis: Verification and monitoring system to detect barrier degradation

supporting_text: The mechanical integrity test on the Segment 5114 pipeline is required every 5 years. The last test that was completed prior to the incident was on September 16, 2015.
Due to the results of Level I inspection, a mechanical integrity inspection of Segment 5114 began on June 29, 2023.

controls.name: Rope access inspection near waterline
controls.barrier_role: Complete mechanical integrity inspection in critical corrosion zones
controls.lod_basis: Verification activity to detect degradation in high-risk areas

supporting_text: Rope access was needed to complete the mechanical integrity inspection near the waterline but that was not performed prior to the incident.
Rope access was needed to complete the mechanical integrity test for Segment 5114 near the waterline but was not performed prior to the incident.

controls.name: Coating maintenance program
controls.barrier_role: Maintain integrity of corrosion protection system
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance to sustain primary barrier effectiveness

supporting_text: the coating was not maintained overtime to prevent corrosion of the underlying steel when exposed

controls.name: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system
controls.barrier_role: Isolate gas source and stop flow upon detection of emergency
controls.lod_basis: Immediate automated/manual response to limit consequence escalation

supporting_text: The Emergency Shut Down (ESD) was activated and instructions were given to the remaining platforms in the SM 130 field to shut in.

controls.name: Firewall separation
controls.barrier_role: Provide physical separation between quarters and production areas
controls.lod_basis: Passive barrier to limit fire spread and protect personnel

supporting_text: As the operators on the quarters side of the firewall walked to the production side, a fire was observed

controls.name: Muster and evacuation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Protect personnel by relocating to safe area
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response procedure to prevent personnel injury

supporting_text: All non-essential personnel mustered across the bridge to the SM 130 D facility.

controls.name: Fire suppression equipment (fire hose and extinguishers)
controls.lod_basis: Active firefighting equipment to control and extinguish fire

supporting_text: Operators utilized the fire hose to soak the smoldering boards under the wet oil tank and four 30 lb. dry chemical fire extinguishers were used to extinguish the lifesaving equipment and other items in the area that caught on fire or were smoldering due to the pipeline fire.

controls.name: Level I pipeline inspection
controls.barrier_role: Initial screening to identify pipelines requiring detailed inspection
controls.lod_basis: Monitoring and verification activity to trigger further assessment

supporting_text: Due to the results of Level I inspection, a mechanical integrity inspection of Segment 5114 began on June 29, 2023.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
